channel.title: Pages on Ana García's Space
channel.description: Recent content in Pages on Ana García's Space

item.title: About me
item.description:
Hello! I’m Ana, and this is my webpage. Here, I will write blog posts about the things I do or the things I think.

This page is the ‘About me’ section, so if you want to know more about me, this is your place!

Introduction

I am 24 years old, and I am proud of navigating the tech industry for nearly three years.

As a passionate Software Engineer, I enjoy designing complex and innovative systems while learning new technologies and ways of working.

Beyond that, I enjoy public speaking and social media writing, skillfully balancing both facets of my personality. That’s why I wanted my page to have a blog section!

Where I'm from

I’m from Asturias, Spain. I have lived in Gijón my whole life and love this city.

Education and experience

I finished my Degree in Software Engineering a year ago in The School of Computer Engineering at the University of Oviedo.

During those years, I participated in student representation, being the president of some organizations during my last years at the University. I also participated in student representation organizations, like RITSI, being part of the board of directors during my last year there. I sometimes still participate by giving talks or helping with projects.

Those experiences gave me confidence in communicating with the team and directing projects. I also learned much about public speaking and getting many people to understand an idea.

Projects

I have created projects using a wide variety of programming languages. I usually use Java at work, but I also used Javascript for my final degree project (a webpage where I created the frontend and backend parts) and Python and C# during the degree. I also had to manage servers and work with PHP, WordPress and Docker while on RITSI.

Apart from that, I have created two projects with some teammates using SOLID.

Hobbies

I’m currently taking theatre classes! I also like painting (an old hobby I’m getting back into), and sometimes I go play volleyball with friends (I’m not good, but I give my best).

I also like taking walks, going to cafeterias and meeting friends. I played role games in the past, too!

What is 'anuskuskus'?

That’s my nickname. One of my favourite teachers gave it to me in school, and I started using it for social media. Sometimes, people refer to me as it, so my boyfriend bought this domain for me. It is unique; why not use it?

Fun fact: sometimes, I can’t use it on pages because the algorithms detect bad words on it… So I have to use ‘kuskus’ instead :’)

How to contact me

I am active on LinkedIn, so If you want to reach me quickly, that’s the best place to go :) I’m open to talking about anything, so if you are curious about a project, post or want to know me better, I’ll be happy to answer.

You can reach me in Spanish or English, and we can give it a try to communicate in Asturian if you want!

item.title: My talks
item.description:
I like participating in events, videos and giving talks; sometimes, those talks are recorded and uploaded on the internet, so I will put the links here so you can enjoy them.

Relevance is not a Thing but a Perception

My first talk in a Tech event! I participated in Berlin Buzzwords 2022 edition and it was a pleasure.

Description

When talking about relevance regarding search, it often sounds like it is a thing, something that can be touched and seen. Nevertheless, that is not the case. What do I mean by that? In this talk, I will provide some examples of how relevance is often merely seen as a score when it can be, in fact, an engaging relationship where the user and the search UI connect in aesthetic and enjoyable ways. I will present numerous examples of innovative search experiences that challenge prevailing schemas and structures and lead instead to elements of motion and correlated visual action that allows us to perceive the beauty of relevancy on a different level. Because relevance is a matter of perception.

Recording

A different way of thinking: non-technical introduction to DSLs

My talk at ShuttleCon First Edition

Description

On this talk I explain how to solve a problem using a DSL. I also introduce the public about what a DSL is.

Recording

You can see my talk at 1:08:36

Ethics, Data, Experiences - Bridging the gap between innovation and responsibility

I participated in the Ethics in eCommerce Summit in London 2023 - May 16th.

Ramiro Álvarez, Andreas Wagemann and I prepared this short talk, and it was a fun challenge to work with people with different backgrounds than me!

Description

We claim that there is a gap between technical curiosity and ethical boundaries. Technically and intellectually, using statistical models to infer human behaviour poses interesting challenges. They can provide insightful inferences, and the fast-paced development leads to rapid increase in use-cases.

Recording

You can see the recording on the ECA Summit page
